description: Retroactive permit for previously unpermitted work per 12/05/24 Violation Letter Case No. CE 240560. SUMMARY: 1. Conversion of 1 bathroom to 2 bathrooms 2. Replacement of 2 windows (sliding doors) 3. Installation of canned lighting (replaced 1 fixture with 2 fixtures). 4. Enlarge passage opening between kitchen and dining area. ---DETAILS--- Main/Hall bathroom Remodel PREVIOUS: bathroom was a full bathroom with a toilet, vanity, tub and shower. CURRENT: bathroom is a full bathroom with a toilet, vanity and tub. The main bathroom shower and hall closet area was converted to enlarge the master bathroom. Main bathroom remodel: Installed new tub, toilet, vanity and fixtures. Standard tub size (30' X 60") with tile surround. Installed new flooring. Installed new light fixture over the vanity. Installed exhaust fan. Master Bathroom Enlarged and Remodeled PREVIOUS: bathroom at time of home purchase was a half bath with a toilet and vanity. CURRENT: bathroom is a full bathroom with toilet, vanity and shower. The main bathroom shower and hall closet area was converted to enlarge the master bathroom. Master bathroom remodel: Installed new toilet, vanity and fixtures. Installed new 36" X 43" tiled shower Installed new flooring. Installed new light fixture over vanity. Installed exhaust fan. Replace broken sliding glass door in family room same size and location Window Details: Jeld-Wen brand Horizontal slider Rough opening 60" W x 80" H) 59.5" X 79.5" Color: White Glazing: Sun resist Argon: Yes UV Factor .28 Replace window in dining room and kitchen same size and location Window Details: Anderson brand Rough opening 80" X 104.5" Window size: 79.25 X 104" Operation: Fixed/Left Stationary Color: White Argon: Yes UV Factor: A1 0.29, A2 0.31 Installation of canned lighting PREVIOUS: dining area had 1 light fixture located in the dining room area approx 93" from sliding door wall. CURRENT: dining area has 2 canned light fixtures (white) The new light fixtures were installed at 40" on center from the side walls and 94" from the sliding glass door. Enlarge passage opening between kitchen and dining room PREVIOUS: Opening between the kitchen and dining area was 60" (5'). CURRENT: Opening between the kitchen and dining is 120" (10'6"). The new opening was built with a 4" x 12" header and double 2" X 4" supports on each side.

In addition, the existing fireplace and chimney were removed and we are proposing a new fireplace insert at a new location.

description: Modification Description Golden Home Access is proposing the installation of a straight stairlift to assist the residents of 1857 Golden Rain Road, Manor 8 with safe and independent access between floors. The stairlift will be installed along the exterior staircase of the unit and is designed to comply with all manufacturer specifications and safety standards. Scope of Work ? Type of Equipment: Access BDD HomeGlide ? Location: Staircase for Manor 8 ? Mounting Method: The stairlift rail will be secured directly to the stairs, not the wall, using standard mounting brackets. ? Work Duration: Installation will take approximately 4-6 hours during a single day ? Noise/Disruption: Work will be completed during normal business hours. Noise will be minimal and limited to light drilling and hand tools. ? Safety & Compliance: The installation will meet ADA guidelines where applicable and manufacturer safety specifications. The equipment is UL-listed and includes seat belts, obstruction sensors, and emergency stop functions. ? Reversibility: The stairlift can be fully removed without permanent damage to the stairs. Any holes from mounting brackets will be patched, and stairs can be returned to their original condition. ? The stairlift is for personal, non-commercial use only. Contact & Insurance Golden Home Access is a licensed, bonded, and insured contractor. Proof of insurance and relevant credentials can be provided upon request. Please contact us with any questions or additional requirements.

description: To enhance substation security and improve NERC regulation compliance, PG&E proposes to replace and upgrade the perimeter fence surrounding Lakewood Substation in Contra Costa County, California. LCreek. PG&E plans to remove the eastern, western, and southern perimeter fence lines and associated foundations, and replace them with portions of pre-cast concrete wall and chain-link fencing. Specifically, the northeastern fence line will be replaced with approximately 275’ of 10’ high pre-cast concrete wall offset approximately 10’ from the existing fence line. The southeastern fence line will be replaced and expanded to the eastern property line with approximately 254’ of 8’ high chain-link fence. The southern fence line will be replaced and expanded to the south by installing approximately 288’ of 8’ high chain-link fence offset approximately 4’ from the existing fence line. The western fence line will be replaced and expanded to the west by installing approximately 564’ of 8’ high chain-link fence offset approximately 4’ from the existing fence line. The total length of replaced fence line will be approximately 1,300 feet, and the total length of new fence and concrete wall will be approximately 1,400 feet. The existing 26’ wide gate along the eastern fence line will be replaced with a new 26’ wide, 8’ tall gate and combined foundation to support the new wall and gate post. The existing 18’ wide gate on the southeastern fence line will be removed and replaced with a 26’ wide, 8’ high gate along the southern fence line. This will allow access from Ygnacio Valley Road. The existing man gate and associated foundations at the northwest corner of the substation will be replaced with a new 4’ wide, 8’ high man gate. PG&E will also replace the existing eroded ditch along the northeast side of the substation and south of the oil retention pond with new ditch. Existing fencing and gates will remain until new fencing, gates, and walls are erected. New fencing and walls will also have anti-dig barriers underneath them. PG&E is coordinating with the City of Walnut Creek to acquire a fence permit for this work. Additionally, PG&E will grade approximately 0.25 acres of land in support of this work predominantly in the southern portion of the substation to ensure proper drainage. Vegetation management involved with this project will include the removal and trimming of some oak trees on the property, as well as the protection and re-plantation of vegetation along the proposed fence and wall lines. This will be performed by PG&E Vegetation Management. Equipment during construction may include various types of rubber-wheeled construction trucks, backhoes, graders, haulers, cement mixer, and miscellaneous hand tools. Construction is anticipated to begin in 2026.

permit_type: Building Revision to Issued Permit
description: Structural drawings were revised following drawing approval to incorporate additional details necessary for construction of the RF shielding room for the UCSF MCH W 3.0T MRI Replacement project. A new sheet, S51-03, was added to consolidate structural details for the RF shielding room that were previously issued as structural sketches in response to contractor issued RFIs. These details include RF shielding support, cable tray support, soffit framing, utility supports within the RF shielding enclosure, and a revised equipment anchorage detail for the chiller interface panel. In addition to the inclusion of S51-03, revisions were made to sheet S51-02. Detail 3 on S51-02 was removed from the drawing set because updated RF shielding support details were incorporated into S51-03. Detail 5 on S51-02 was also removed following the issuance, review, and approval of RFI 013, which directed the project to follow ETS Lindgren’s finish wall anchorage detailing. Furthermore, Details 1 and 2 on S51-02 were revised to reflect anchorage locations measured from the isocenter rather than the center of gravity, as the original configuration was not constructible.to B241425
